board.designation: GH-DB — Gate-house sub-DB, Nairobi industrial light-engineering workshop
board.manufacturer_class: KS Annex E (BS EN 61439-3 Distribution Board)

incoming_supply.fed_from: MSP-100 C08 (40A MCB Type B + 30mA Type A RCD, 60m submain)

selectivity_results.code_clause: KS 1700:2018 § 536 (route to BS 7671 via §313) — 40A Type B upstream cascading to 6-16A Type B at GH-DB (current ratio ≥2.5:1) satisfies same-curve cascade selectivity at 1.0 kA local PFC. RCD selectivity caveat: upstream 30mA Type A at MSP-100 C08 and downstream 30mA Type A on C02/C03 share sensitivity — KS 1700:2018 §314 (route to BS 7671 §314.1) requires staged sensitivities for full RCD selectivity. Coincident tripping accepted by design because the upstream RCD exclusively serves the gate-house submain; an earth fault tripping both is operationally acceptable for the isolated perimeter load and avoids inventing a 100mA upstream RCD that the existing MSP-100 schedule does not carry.

assumptions: KS 1700:2018 Annex E adopts BS EN 61439-3 for distribution boards; KS Annex E adopts BS Table 41.2 disconnection-time tables verbatim.
KS 1700:2018 §411.3.3 universal socket-RCD policy: 30mA Type A applied on C02 (socket) and C03 (comms outlet — small DC content from CCTV switching supplies).
Conservative PFC at GH-DB ≈ 1.0 kA after the 60m 10mm² Cu SWA submain impedance (loop Z ≈ 0.24 Ω) from 9.8 kA declared at MSP-100; 6 kA Icn on the GH-DB MCBs provides >5x headroom.
Single-phase 240V intake — gate house is a small isolated load best fed as single-phase to simplify isolation and minimise neutral imbalance at MSP-100.

drawing_notes: GH-DB located inside the gate-house plant cupboard at the site perimeter.
Fed from MSP-100 C08 via 60m 10mm² 4-core SWA PVC Cu submain.
IP54 enclosure rating suits semi-outdoor gate-house cupboard.

rationale.chat_summary: GH-DB: 40A single-phase 240V gate-house sub-DB, 3 circuits (gate lighting + sockets + CCTV/intercom), fed from MSP-100 C08 via 60m 10mm² Cu SWA submain. KS Annex E (BS EN 61439-3) IP54 enclosure suits semi-outdoor perimeter cupboard. 30mA Type A RCD on C02/C03 per KS 1700:2018 §411.3.3 socket policy. Conservative 1.0 kA local PFC after submain attenuation (loop Z ≈ 0.24 Ω) from 9.8 kA at MSP-100; 6 kA Icn MCBs provide >5x headroom. Selectivity to MSP-100 cascade verified at 2.5:1 ratio; coincident-RCD caveat accepted because upstream 30mA serves only this gate-house submain.

sections.title: Board Identification
sections.summary: GH-DB is the dedicated gate-house sub-DB for the Nairobi industrial light-engineering workshop, housed in the gate-house plant cupboard at the site perimeter. Role flagged as `gate_house_distribution` so downstream consumers recognise it as an isolated perimeter board with environmental exposure (IP54 enclosure for semi-outdoor tropical conditions) and limited-load scope. Enclosure built to KS Annex E which adopts BS EN 61439-3 verbatim for distribution-board construction in Kenya.

decisions.label: Board role flagged as gate_house_distribution
decisions.summary: Signals isolated perimeter scope and IP54 environmental constraint to downstream skills (riser, cable-containment, earthing).

decisions.label: IP54 enclosure for semi-outdoor gate-house cupboard
decisions.summary: Gate-house cupboards in Nairobi face dust ingress + occasional wind-driven rain through ventilation louvres; IP54 protects against dust deposition and splash water without forcing the cost of an IP65 outdoor pillar.
decisions.rule: Enclosure IP selection for tropical semi-outdoor
decisions.code_clause: KS Annex E (BS EN 61439-3 Distribution Board) + IEC 60529:2013

sections.title: Incoming Supply
sections.summary: 40A single-phase 240V intake fed from MSP-100 C08 via 60m 10mm² 4-core SWA PVC Cu submain. Single-phase chosen because the gate-house load is small (~2.3 kW total) and isolated — TPN would force neutral imbalance management at MSP-100 with no engineering benefit. Submain impedance (loop Z ≈ 0.24 Ω for 60m 10mm² Cu SWA) conservatively attenuates PFC from 9.8 kA at MSP-100 to ≈1.0 kA at GH-DB; well within the 6 kA Icn of the downstream MCBs with >5x headroom. Upstream 30mA Type A RCD at MSP-100 C08 protects the submain itself per KS 1700:2018 §411.3.3.

decisions.label: Single-phase 240V intake
decisions.summary: Small isolated load (~2.3 kW) — single-phase simplifies isolation and avoids neutral imbalance at MSP-100.
decisions.rule: Phase selection by load size and topology

decisions.label: Declared PFC 1.0 kA conservative estimate after 60m submain
decisions.summary: Conservative attenuation from 9.8 kA at MSP-100 via 10mm² Cu SWA submain impedance (loop Z ≈ 0.24 Ω); covered by 6 kA Icn MCBs at GH-DB with >5x headroom.
decisions.code_clause: KS 1700:2018 Appendix 4 (route to BS 7671 Appendix 4) + IEC 60909-0

decisions.label: Upstream 30mA Type A RCD at MSP-100 C08
decisions.summary: Protects the 60m submain itself and provides additional discrimination layer for the perimeter gate-house circuit per KS 1700:2018 §411.3.3.
decisions.rule: RCD on submain serving isolated outbuilding

sections.title: Circuit Breakdown
sections.summary: 3 final circuits: C01 gate-house lighting (external floodlights + internal, 10A Type B, no RCD) + C02 general sockets (16A Type B, 30mA Type A RCD) + C03 comms outlet for CCTV + intercom (6A Type B, 30mA Type A RCD). Type B curve selected throughout: lighting is incandescent/LED with no inrush, sockets feed general appliances with no specialist motor inrush, and CCTV/intercom DC content is small enough that Type A RCD coverage is adequate. Diversity not applied at GH-DB — total connected ~2.3 kW well within 40A intake.

decisions.label: Type B MCBs throughout
decisions.summary: Lighting (LED/incandescent), general sockets, and small CCTV/intercom loads have no specialist inrush — Type B is the lowest-cost compliant curve.
decisions.rule: MCB curve selection by load type

decisions.label: 30mA Type A RCD on C02 (socket) and C03 (CCTV/intercom)
decisions.summary: KS 1700:2018 §411.3.3 universal socket-RCD policy; Type A handles pulsating DC residual from CCTV switching supplies.

decisions.label: No RCD on C01 gate-house lighting
decisions.summary: Fixed external floodlights — outdoor luminaires considered under § 714 (outdoor lighting installations). Class II construction OR luminaires not within touch reach satisfies § 714 without forcing 30mA RCD; final-circuit MCB provides overcurrent and short-circuit protection.

sections.title: Selectivity Analysis
sections.summary: Upstream MSP-100 C08 = 40A Type B MCB + 30mA Type A RCD. Downstream MCBs at GH-DB are 6-16A Type B. Cascade ratio ≥2.5:1 satisfies same-curve cascade selectivity at the 1.0 kA local PFC per KS 1700:2018 §536. RCD coordination caveat: the upstream 30mA Type A at MSP-100 C08 shares sensitivity with the downstream 30mA Type A at C02/C03 — series 30mA pairs are time-coincident and KS 1700:2018 §314 requires staged sensitivities for true RCD selectivity. Coincident tripping accepted by design because the upstream RCD exclusively serves the gate-house submain; an earth fault tripping both is operationally acceptable for an isolated perimeter load.

decisions.label: MCB cascade selectivity at 2.5:1 ratio
decisions.summary: 40A:16A worst-case ratio satisfies IEC 60898 same-curve cascade threshold (≥2:1) at 1.0 kA local PFC.

decisions.label: Coincident 30mA RCD pair accepted by design
decisions.summary: Upstream and downstream 30mA Type A pair is non-selective by KS 1700:2018 §314, but accepted because the upstream RCD serves only this submain — no other downstream consumers are penalised by coincident trip.
decisions.rule: RCD selectivity exemption by upstream-RCD-scope analysis

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: GH-DB compliant against KS 1700:2018 (the Kenyan adoption of the IET Wiring Regulations, with §313 establishing equivalence to BS 7671:2018), KS Annex E (BS EN 61439-3 distribution-board construction), BS EN 60898-1:2019 (MCBs), and BS EN 61009-1:2012 (RCBOs where applied). One INFO note: coincident 30mA RCD pair with MSP-100 C08 — not a non-compliance but a deliberate design acceptance documented in Selectivity Analysis. No CRITICAL or HIGH flags raised.

decisions.label: KS 1700:2018 + BS 7671 dual-routing citation
decisions.summary: Every protection-related clause cites KS 1700:2018 with `(route to BS 7671 via §313)` qualifier — KS 1700 §313 establishes BS 7671:2018 equivalence and the IET Wiring Regs are the deeper-published source.

decisions.label: Enclosure construction per KS Annex E
decisions.summary: KS Annex E adopts BS EN 61439-3 verbatim for distribution-board enclosures — selection driven by IP54 environmental classification.
decisions.code_clause: KS Annex E (BS EN 61439-3 Distribution Board)

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Schedule emitted as 3-row table with 1 spare way (W4 reserved). Spare-way provision allows for typical gate-house growth: barrier-motor circuit, additional perimeter floodlight string, or guard-room small-power upgrade. Tropical-environment derating implicit in the 6 kA Icn margin and IP54 enclosure choice; no per-circuit derating applied because the cupboard interior temperature is moderated by ventilation and the 60m feeder is below the 70°C ground-laid cable threshold.

decisions.label: 1 spare way reserved (W4)
decisions.summary: Typical gate-house growth path: barrier motor, perimeter floodlight extension, or guard-room small-power upgrade.

invariants.evidence: INV-14 board GH-DB (KE → BS): R1 jurisdiction→format mapping (GB/KE → BS 7671 §514) holds; R2 board_label.text+svg populated, no {{}} remnants; R3 board_label.text matches BS format regex; R4 3/3 circuits carry circuit_label.text+svg, no {{}} remnants; R5 every circuit_label.text matches BS circuit regex; R6 tool_call_pending booleans set on all labels.
